characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Category Traitor: Most of the APD views Will as a rat because he exposed corruption within the department. Cool Car: Will drives a Porsche Targa that he restored himself. Covered in Scars: Will's body is covered in scars due to years of abuse in group homes and foster care. One on his neck is visible at all times even when he's wearing collared shirts. Dark and Troubled Past:His prostitute mother Lucy was murdered in the 1980s by a serial killer. "L" Is for "Dyslexia": Will is severely dyslexic and struggles to read even with the help of specialized lenses. He keeps his condition a secret from the GBI and relies on audio notes to keep track of his cases.

characters.name: Will Trent
characters.description: Category Traitor: Most of the APD views Will as a rat because he exposed corruption within the department. Cool Car: Will drives a Porsche Targa that he restored himself. Covered in Scars: Will's body is covered in scars due to years of abuse in group homes and foster care. One on his neck is visible at all times even when he's wearing collared shirts. Dark and Troubled Past:His prostitute mother Lucy was murdered in the 1980s by a serial killer. "L" Is for "Dyslexia": Will is severely dyslexic and struggles to read even with the help of specialized lenses. He keeps his condition a secret from the GBI and relies on audio notes to keep track of his cases.

characters.name: Faith Mitchell
characters.description: Absurdly Youthful Mother: Discussed in the pilot when Will meets Jeremy, Faith's college-aged son. Will is surprised she could have a son so mature, but it is later explained that Faith had aTeen Pregnancy, justifying the trope. Action Girl: A seasoned APD officer before she joined the GBI. New Meat: She was recently transferred from the APD to the GBI. Over time, she becomes a seasoned GBI agent.

characters.name: Amanda Wagner
characters.description: Breaking the Glass Ceiling: Started her career getting put down by white male detectives inThe '80s, alongside Evelyn, but rose through the ranks to become Deputy Director of the Georgia Bureau of Investigations. Like a Son to Me: For Will. Amanda was the first person to really believe in him, despite his past, and took him under her wing. Turned out to go even deeper when Will finds out Amanda is the one who found him in the trash can as a baby, took him in asYoung Witness Caretaking, and tried to adopt him, naming him after her own parents. Reasonable Authority Figure: She has no problem keeping Will working under the GBI despite his dyslexic condition.

characters.name: Angie Polaski
characters.description: Dark and Troubled Past: Was in multiple foster homes with a case of sexual abuse involving her and a foster parent.

characters.name: Michael Ormewood
characters.description: Badges and Dog Tags: Served in Afghanistan with the US military (implied to be Army) before he got a discharge and went to Atlanta to join the APD.

characters.name: Betty
characters.description: Canine Companion: Betty, Will's precious dog. He didn't want her, at first, but he quickly warms up to her and fusses over her well-being and is prepared to put out a dragnet when he believes she's gone missing. Named After Somebody Famous: SpecificallyBetty White.

characters.name: Max and Cooper Ormewood
characters.description: Gender-Blender Name: Cooper is a gender neutral name, but one that was more associated with boys until relatively recently.

characters.name: Lucy Morales
characters.description: Death by Childbirth: Discussed. While Lucy died in childbirth, whichbriefly makes Will feel like he is the one who killed her, Amanda reminds him that she wouldn't have been giving birth tied up in a dirty room without a doctor had it not been forJames Ulster. Death by Origin Story: Without Lucy dying, Will would not have been orphaned and put in the foster system. Disposable Sex Worker: Deconstructed. Lucy was considered just one of many victims of a mysterious serial killer inThe '80s, though one who was kept the longest, found last, and not technically killed by the killer. Because of her background, the homicide detectives at the time were not thorough in their investigations. However, flashbacks in "Nothing Changed Except For Everything" show that Amanda and Evelyn were fond and protective of Lucy since she was on their beat, and she turns out to beConnected All Alongto Will, as she is revealed to be his biological mother. Posthumous Character: The character is only seen in flashbacks and hallucinations, as she died decades prior to the series. Who's Your Daddy?: Lucy tells Angela and Evelyn that one of her clients is the father of her baby. Considering she was a prostitute, it's implied even she didn't know who the father was.Considering the later reveal that itwasn'ta client at all, this makes the implication even stronger.

characters.name: Antonio Miranda
characters.description: "L" Is for "Dyslexia": Like Will, Antonio has severe dyslexia, something they bond over.

characters.name: Seth
characters.description: Contrasting New Love Interest: Where Will is buttoned-up and shares a complicated history with Angie, Seth is warm and open, helping Angie forget her tragedies.

characters.name: Paul Campano
characters.description: Schoolyard Bully All Grown Up: Exaggerated, as Paul bullied Will in their shared home rather than just at school. They start out with a contentious relationship, but start to mend some fences throughout Season 1.

characters.name: Nico
characters.description: Gleeful and Grumpy Pairing: Generally when playing off of Will in a scene. Kaleidoscope Hair: They first had blue hair, later changing to dark hair with blond streaks. No Last Name Given: Nico only ever goes by Nico.

characters.name: Rafael Wexford
characters.description: Gayngster: A gang boss who is openly gay, paints his nails, and is scary when you cross him.

characters.name: Marion Alba
characters.description: Contrasting New Love Interest: After Will fell out with Angie, who is cynical and complicated, Marion is sweet and uncomplicated.
